description: Technology detectors and Skills for the AIEF Adoption Engine. This is data, not workflow logic. Three distinct concepts: a DETECTOR fires on project signals (dependencies, files, keywords); a SKILL RECOMMENDATION maps fired detectors ('when') to a Skill; SKILL CONTENT (purpose, whenToUse, standardsToRead, promptContext, commonRisks, evidenceExpectations) is contextual knowledge injected into prompts — AIEF includes it as context, it does not execute Skills.

skills.name: Multitenant SaaS Architect
skills.description: Tenant isolation, Host header resolution, tenant lifecycle, SaaS architecture.
skills.whenToUse: Any Change that touches tenant resolution, tenant-scoped data or tenant lifecycle.
skills.promptContext: This is a multitenant system: every data access must be tenant-scoped, tenant resolution (e.g. by Host header or subdomain) is security-critical, and cross-tenant leakage is a critical bug.
commonRisks: shared caches leaking data across tenants
skills.evidenceExpectations: Evidence should state how tenant isolation was verified for anything touched.

skills.name: n8n Automation Ops
skills.description: n8n Queue Mode, workflow catalog, executions, workers, Redis, health checks.
skills.whenToUse: Changes that call the n8n API, manage workflows/executions, or affect automation reliability.
skills.promptContext: n8n is an external system: calls to its API must handle timeouts and failures explicitly, workflow activation is a privileged operation, and executions carry potentially sensitive payloads.
commonRisks: logging execution payloads with sensitive data
skills.evidenceExpectations: Evidence should record how n8n interactions were tested (mock or real instance).

skills.name: AWS SaaS Platform
skills.description: Cognito, RDS PostgreSQL, Secrets Manager, CloudFront, WAF, CloudWatch, CodePipeline, Terraform.
skills.whenToUse: Changes involving AWS services, authentication flows or infrastructure configuration.
skills.promptContext: AWS-managed services are configured, not reimplemented: validate tokens server-side (e.g. Cognito JWTs), read secrets from the environment/secret manager, and treat infrastructure changes as reviewable code.
skills.evidenceExpectations: Evidence should note which AWS interactions were exercised for real vs mocked.

skills.name: Security & RBAC Reviewer
skills.description: Server-side authorization, permissions, tenant isolation, secrets, audit events.
skills.whenToUse: Any Change touching authentication, authorization, permissions or sensitive data.
skills.promptContext: Authorization is enforced server-side on every operation; the UI is never a security boundary. Check permissions at the point of data access, not only at the route entry.
commonRisks: authorization checked in the UI but not the API
secrets or tokens ending up in logs or evidence files
skills.evidenceExpectations: Evidence should list which permission paths were tested, including a denied-access case.

skills.name: Next.js / NestJS Architecture
skills.description: Frontend/backend separation, server components, APIs, NestJS modules, adapter patterns.
skills.whenToUse: Changes that cross the frontend/backend boundary or add routes, server components or modules.
skills.promptContext: Keep the server/client boundary explicit: secrets and privileged calls stay server-side, client components receive plain serializable props, and API contracts are validated at the boundary.
commonRisks: server-only code or secrets imported into client bundles
data fetching duplicated on both sides of the boundary
skills.evidenceExpectations: Evidence should state how the affected routes/components were exercised.

skills.name: AI Workflow Governance
skills.description: AI-generated workflows, human approval, inactive drafts, governance, auditability.
skills.whenToUse: Changes where AI generates or modifies artifacts that affect runtime behavior.
skills.promptContext: AI-generated artifacts start inactive and require human approval before affecting runtime; every generation/approval should be attributable and auditable.
commonRisks: no record of who approved what
skills.evidenceExpectations: Evidence should show the approval path for anything AI-generated.

skills.name: Project Architecture Reviewer
skills.description: General architecture review, module boundaries, risks, and technical debt.
skills.whenToUse: When no specific technology signals are detected and a general review is the safest starting point.
